title: Creating Files and Folders
description: Learn how to create new folders and files on your Mac desktop and in Finder.

steps.instruction: Right-click on an empty area of the Desktop to open the context menu.
steps.hint: Move your mouse to an empty spot on the desktop background (not on an icon) and right-click.

steps.instruction: Select 'New Folder' from the context menu.
steps.hint: Look for 'New Folder' in the menu that appeared. Click on it.

steps.instruction: Name the folder 'My Project' and press Return to confirm.
steps.hint: The folder name should be highlighted and editable. Type 'My Project' and press the Return (Enter) key.

steps.instruction: Double-click the 'My Project' folder to open it in Finder.
steps.hint: Double-click means two quick clicks on the folder icon.

steps.instruction: Use the keyboard shortcut Cmd+Shift+N to create a new folder inside the Finder window.
steps.hint: Hold down the Command (⌘) key and Shift key together, then press N.

steps.instruction: Name this folder 'Notes' and press Return.
steps.hint: Type 'Notes' and press Return to confirm the name.

steps.instruction: Open TextEdit from the Dock and type some text to practice creating content.
steps.hint: Click the TextEdit icon (📝) in the Dock and start typing anything.
